task: You are a QA agent, you are testing a website for QA issues. Explore visible controls and form flows with short, targeted actions. Keep memory to one short sentence. Include 'QA: ...' in memory only when behavior appears incorrect or unintended (broken control, unintended logic, invalid state transition, or surprising navigation, etc). Do not include a QA note when no issue is observed.

transitions.observation: memory: Starting QA exploration of the storefront; cart is currently empty. | next_goal: Attempt to add 'Explorer Map' to the cart and verify if the cart updates.

transitions.qa_observation: Clicking 'Add to cart' for 'Explorer Map' failed to update the cart correctly; it currently only displays 'QA Tonic'.
transitions.observation: next_goal: Apply the 10% launch discount and verify the cart total updates.
memory: Discount applied. Cart currently contains 'QA Tonic' with a total of $7.20. Proceeding to checkout. | next_goal: Enter email and select delivery speed to place the order.

memory: Order placed successfully. Now testing if adding items to the cart works after an order has been completed. | next_goal: Click 'Add to cart' for 'Focus Beans' to see if the cart updates after an order.

transitions.action_summary: {"done":{"text":"The cart failed to update after clicking 'Add to cart' for 'Focus Beans' following a completed order. The cart continues to display the items from the previous order ('QA Tonic' and 'Explorer Map').","success":false}}

done.text: The cart failed to update after clicking 'Add to cart' for 'Focus Beans' following a completed order. The cart continues to display the items from the previous order ('QA Tonic' and 'Explorer Map').

transitions.qa_observation: Clicking 'Add to cart' for 'Focus Beans' after an order has been placed fails to update the cart, which still displays the items from the previous order.
transitions.observation: next_goal: Report the finding that the cart does not update after an order is placed.
